# Requirements Quality Checklist: Flowguard Production Readiness

**Purpose**: Unit-test the written requirements (completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability)—not implementation behavior—for the flowguard v1.0 production-readiness feature.
**Created**: 2026-03-23
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command. Depth: standard; intended audience: PR / spec reviewer. Focus areas: core limiter & unified config; backend/frontend adapters; NFRs & success criteria.

**Review status**: Items below are satisfied by `spec.md` (including Session 2026-03-23 (b)), `data-model.md`, `plan.md`, and `contracts/` as of 2026-03-23. Re-verify after any material spec change.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 - Are functional requirements enumerated for every adapter named in scope (Express, Node HTTP, Next.js API, Next.js Edge, Koa, React hook, fetch wrapper, Axios) such that no adapter is only implied by user stories? [Completeness, Spec §Scope Boundaries, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK002 - Are requirements stated for raw Node.js HTTP parity with other backend adapters (headers, 429, keyGenerator, skip, onLimit) at the same level of specificity as Express/Koa? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK003 - Does the specification document the public surface of `createRateLimiter` and `RateLimiterResult` fields exhaustively enough that FR-005 cannot be misread (e.g., optional vs required fields, units for `retryAfter`)? [Completeness, Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK004 - Are cleanup/destroy/lifecycle requirements defined for long-running servers and for tests (timers, MemoryStore disposal) where the plan implies `destroy()`-style behavior? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §Key Entities—MemoryStore]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 - Is “human-readable window” parsing unambiguous for allowed tokens—are minimum/maximum bounds, plural forms, and whitespace rules specified or explicitly left implementation-defined? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK006 - Is “wall-clock-aligned” for the fixed window defined precisely enough (boundary semantics, timezone) to avoid divergent implementations? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK007 - Are `RateLimit-Reset` semantics specified as HTTP-date vs seconds (RFC 9110 family ambiguity) so adapters cannot legitimately disagree? [Clarity, Gap, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK008 - Is the default denied-response HTTP status called out as exactly 429 everywhere FR-010 applies, and is configurability bounded (e.g., only status vs also body schema)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK009 - Is “coerce … to safe types” for headers (FR-017) tied to concrete threat classes (injection, non-ASCII) and acceptable representations? [Clarity, Measurability, Spec §FR-017]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK010 - Do user-story acceptance scenarios, functional requirements, and edge cases agree on empty-string key handling vs FR-019 (global fallback vs error path)? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-019]
- [x] CHK011 - Are `maxKeys` / LRU defaults and eviction policy described consistently across FR-007, Key Entities, Assumptions, and Edge Cases (including numeric default)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK012 - Do non-functional bundle-size limits (NFR-PF) align with “zero runtime dependencies in core” and tree-shaking claims without conflicting implied budgets per entry point? [Consistency, Spec §NFR-PF, Spec §User Story 4]
- [x] CHK013 - If supplementary docs (e.g., implementation plan) repeat numeric defaults like `maxKeys`, do they match the specification’s authoritative values? [Consistency, Cross-artifact, Assumption—verify against plan/data-model]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK014 - Can SC-002’s “zero false allows / zero false denies” be objectively verified given concurrency and timing wording (single-process, event-loop serialization)? [Measurability, Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK015 - Are SC-006 and SC-007 framed so they are requirements on instrumentation and measurement rather than uncontrollable outcomes, or are they labeled as aspirational metrics? [Acceptance Criteria Quality, Spec §SC-006, Spec §SC-007]
- [x] CHK016 - Is “under 5 minutes” in SC-001 tied to documented prerequisites (tooling, network) so completion time is a fair measure? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK017 - Are alternate flows defined for partial adapter failure (e.g., `onLimit` throws) versus `keyGenerator` failure (FR-019)—is the distinction preserved in requirements without overlap? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-019]
- [x] CHK018 - Are requirements stated for skipped requests (`skip`) regarding headers, debug output, and whether `onLimit` may fire—across core and adapters? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-014, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK019 - Are multi-tab or worker multi-context scenarios for frontend adapters explicitly in or out of scope for v1.0? [Coverage, Scope, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Scope Boundaries]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK020 - Is clock skew / backward jumps addressed at the same level as forward NTP jumps, or explicitly excluded? [Edge Case Coverage, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK021 - Are requirements defined for MemoryStore behavior when `max` is extremely large relative to `maxKeys` (eviction vs correctness)? [Edge Case Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK022 - Is behavior specified when window duration parses to zero or sub-millisecond values if such strings are allowed? [Edge Case Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-003]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK023 - Are accessibility or internationalization requirements intentionally absent for CLI/messages, or should user-visible error strings meet any baseline? [Non-Functional, Gap, Spec §NFR-UX]
- [x] CHK024 - Is the performance budget (NFR-PF) scoped to named artifacts (core entry vs full package) so “minified+gzipped” is not ambiguous across adapters? [Clarity, Spec §NFR-PF]
- [x] CHK025 - Are coverage thresholds (NFR-TS) linked to exclusion rules (generated files, types-only modules) to keep them measurable in CI? [Measurability, Spec §NFR-TS]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK026 - Is the “trust proxy” assumption for IP-based keys paired with explicit documentation obligations only, or are mis-keyed limits acknowledged as out of scope risks? [Dependency, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-013]
- [x] CHK027 - Are peer dependency version ranges for React/Express/Next/Koa/axios stated as requirements or left to package management only? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §Assumptions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK028 - Does the specification resolve whether optional `debug` output may include PII (e.g., raw keys, IPs) or define redaction expectations? [Ambiguity, Gap, Spec §FR-018]
- [x] CHK029 - Is “namespace internal storage keys” (FR-016) defined with a collision model versus user-supplied key strings across instances? [Clarity, Spec §FR-016]
- [x] CHK030 - Are publishing and CI requirements (User Story 5) free of conflicts with “no implementation details” guidance elsewhere in feature docs—i.e., are npm/CI requirements clearly scoped as delivery constraints? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 5, checklist prerequisites]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Prefer resolving findings in `spec.md` (or linked contracts) before treating an item as satisfied.
- Items reference the feature spec; where marked [Cross-artifact] or [Gap], confirm against `plan.md`, `data-model.md`, and `contracts/`.
